lede: A look at September 26 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Serena Williams and Michael Ballack, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Typhoon Vera
events.desc: Typhoon Vera, the strongest typhoon to hit Japan in recorded history, makes landfall, killing 4,580 people and leaving nearly 1.6 million others homeless.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: Holocaust: Senior SS official August Frank issues a memorandum detailing how Jews should be "evacuated".

events.title: Machine Gun Kelly (gangster)
events.desc: As gangster Machine Gun Kelly surrenders to the FBI, he shouts out, "Don't shoot, G-Men!", which becomes a nickname for FBI agents.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Battle of Polygon Wood begins.

events.title: Albert Einstein
events.desc: Albert Einstein publishes the third of his Annus Mirabilis papers, introducing the special theory of relativity.

events.title: George Washington
events.desc: George Washington appoints Thomas Jefferson the first United States Secretary of State.

events.title: Francis Drake
events.desc: Francis Drake finishes his circumnavigation of the Earth in Plymouth, England.

events.title: Stephen, King of England
events.desc: Stephen of Blois's army approached Oxford, beginning the siege of Oxford.

events.title: William II of England
events.desc: William II is crowned King of England, and reigns until 1100.

events.title: Julius Caesar
events.desc: Julius Caesar dedicates a temple to Venus Genetrix, fulfilling a vow he made at the Battle of Pharsalus.

births.name: Pope Paul VI
births.line: Head of the Catholic Church from 1963 to 1978

births.name: T. S. Eliot
births.line: English poet, playwright, critic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Manmohan Singh
births.line: Indian economist and politician, 13th Prime Minister of India

births.name: Ivan Pavlov
births.line: Russian physiologist and physician, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Jacques Chirac, French politician, President of France
Paul Newman, American actor, director, producer, and businessman
August Ferdinand Möbius, German mathematician and astronomer
Stephen III, Duke of Bavaria, Duke of Bavaria-Ingolstadt from 1375

observances.text: Christian feast days: Canadian Martyrs (Catholic Church in Canada)
